Age: Vorraki reach adulthood around 16 years and commonly live 60 to 80 years. Visible aging varies by lineage, health, environment, and occupation.
Size Summary: Vorraki typically stand between 6'0" and 7'2". Their size is Medium.
Vision:
You can see in dim light within 60 feet
as if it were bright light, and in
darkness as if it were dim light.
Trait 1: Relentless Endurance|When reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you drop to 1 hit points instead (once per long rest).
Trait 2: Brute Precision|When you make a Strength (Athletics) check, you can double your proficiency bonus (once per short or long rest).
Trait 3: Calculated Violence|Add your Strength modifier to initiative instead of Dexterity.
Trait 4: Commanding Presence|You gain proficiency in Intimidation or Persuasion (your choice).
Trait 5: Tactical Efficiency|You can use the Help action as a bonus action.
Trait 6: Commanding Voice|You gain proficiency in Insight.

Name: Weftilestes
Slogan: Knowledge must be guided, or it will unravel the world.
Quote: “Reality is not broken. It is unfinished.”
Intro: Weftilestes are medium people descended from Weft,Moeder. Their communities are known for controlled research, peer review, accurate records, and restrictions on dangerous experiments. Their unusual physical traits are treated as inherited anatomy, training concerns, or medical needs rather than as symbols detached from daily life.
Origin: Weftilestes descend from Moeder populations that settled near stable Weft research sites. Repeated exposure produced inherited tessellated skin, geometric pupils, and sensitivity to magical structures. Their institutions track descendance through family ledgers, research permissions, apprenticeship lines, and records of exposure incidents.
Appearance: Weftilestes are medium humanoids, typically standing 5'4" to 6'2", with a slender and unnaturally graceful build. They have a refined humanoid skull, fine and geometric brows, a straight and refined nose, and a narrow and symmetrical jaw. Their 2 geometric or fractal eyes are commonly silver, grey, fractal black. Their skin is opalescent and subtly tessellated, with ivory, silver-grey, pale lapis, or iridescent coloration and faint geometric grids and tessellations. Hair is fine, spectral, and silver-white and they have human-like ears. They have 2 arms and 2 legs, with humanoid proportions, five-fingered hands, and plantigrade feet. Their posture is still and precise. Typical grooming: Spectral hair worn loose or arranged in deliberate geometric patterns. Common ornamentation includes geometric jewelry, scholarly robes, tesseral symbols, and carefully curated arcane tools.
Culture & Identity: Weftilestes culture centers on controlled research, peer review, accurate records, and restrictions on dangerous experiments. Children are trained through households, work groups, schools, or orders that teach the habits needed for their expected duties. Status is usually tied to reliable service, visible competence, and whether a person protects the community's records, routes, property, or people.
Societal Roles: Weftilestes commonly serve as arcanists, anomaly scholars, archivists, investigators, spell theorists, ritual geometers, ward designers, memory cataloguers, and advisors on unstable magical phenomena. They are hired to examine damaged wards, unusual ruins, and disputed magical evidence.
Naming Conventions: Weftilestes names are structured and often tied to scholarly lineage, academy affiliation, or arcane specialty. Given names include Serathis, Kaedrin, Tholmir, Xelros, Ormari, Vaelren, Iskalia, Saeryn, Lysithea, Xyrelle, Vesmira, and Thessa. Formal introductions may include a thesis, field, or sponsoring school.
Life in Orimond: In Orimond, Weftilestes work in regulated laboratories, archives, universities, investigative offices, and workshops that maintain magical infrastructure. They are expected to document risks clearly because their work can affect whole districts.
Playstyle & Roleplaying: A Weftilestes character brings training shaped by controlled research, peer review, accurate records, and restrictions on dangerous experiments. Decide which household, order, route, office, or settlement taught you, what obligation still follows you, and what practical problem forced you into the current adventure.
Age: Weftilestes reach adulthood around 40 years and commonly live 200 to 300 years. Visible aging varies by lineage, health, environment, and occupation.
Alignement: Weftislestes are neutral, valuing knowledge and the pursuit of understanding above morality or law.
Size Summary: Weftilestes typically stand between 5'4" and 6'2". Their size is Medium.
Trait 1: Perception Beyond the Veil|You can sense nearby magical distortions, invisible creatures, or anomalies within 30 feet, though you do not always understand them. You have advantage on Intelligence (Arcana) and Investigation checks related to Weft anomalies or dimensional fluctuations.
Trait 2: Weftwoven Memory|You can recall any event you have personally experienced with perfect clarity, unless your memory has been intentionally altered. You gain proficiency in one Intelligence-based skill of your choice: Arcana, History, or Investigation.
Trait 3: Tesseral Reflex|Once per short or long rest, when a creature makes an attack against you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on that attack as you shift a fraction of a second ahead of impact.
Trait 4: Fractal Mind|You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would charm or frighten you, or otherwise cloud your mind.

Name: Zakariss
Intro: Zakariss are medium people descended from Veil. Their communities are known for territorial control, military strength, stable fortifications, and enforced obligations. Their unusual physical traits are treated as inherited anatomy, training concerns, or medical needs rather than as symbols detached from daily life.
Origin: Zakariss descend from Veil-native populations that developed binding sigils to prevent bodily dissolution. Lineages that maintained larger, more stable bodies gained control of fortresses, war roads, and Veil cores. Descendance is recorded through earned sigils, held territory, and recognition by rival commanders.
Appearance: Zakariss are medium humanoids, typically standing 7'0" to 9'0", with a towering, broad, and massively muscular build. They have a heavy and rune-scarred skull, massive and rune-scarred brows, a broad and stone-like nose, and a heavy and capable of splitting wide jaw. Their 2 burning glyph-like eyes are commonly molten white, amber, glyph-lit. Their skin is half-solid war-stone and scarred hide, with basalt black, storm-grey, or rune-lit bronze coloration and pulsing spiral sigils and unstable light seams. Hair is absent, they have human-like ears, they bear crystalline secondary teeth and stone ridges, and their secondary covering consists of rune-bound veil matter. They have 2 arms and 2 legs, with humanoid proportions, five-fingered hands, and plantigrade feet. Their posture is dominant and braced. Typical grooming: Sigils exposed, reinforced, and maintained; unstable seams bound rather than concealed. Common ornamentation includes rune-bound armor, earned conquest sigils, dominion symbols, and seized Veil cores.
Culture & Identity: Zakariss culture centers on territorial control, military strength, stable fortifications, and enforced obligations. Children are trained through households, work groups, schools, or orders that teach the habits needed for their expected duties. Status is usually tied to reliable service, visible competence, and whether a person protects the community's records, routes, property, or people.
Societal Roles: Zakariss most often become warlords, fortress-builders, Veil conquerors, shock troops, siege-breakers, phase-hunters, spell-renders, and commanders of unstable dominions. Some become core-takers, hunting the resources needed to stabilize their drifting fortresses. Others specialize in breaking teleportation, countering phase-shifts, or anchoring hostile terrain long enough for armies to cross. In mixed realms, they are feared as mercenaries, living weapons, and dangerous specialists against Veil phenomena. They are rarely trusted, but often hired when the problem is too large, too unstable, or too violent for subtler peoples to solve.
Naming Conventions: Zakariss are not given true names at birth or manifestation. They begin with guttural designations, battle-sounds, or rune-marks such as Zhak, Vross, Khar, Drekh, Uzzor, Thraal, Korrzh, or Mek. A true name is carved through conquest, endurance, and recognition by rivals. Earned names often reference broken horizons, seized cores, shattered gates, or dominions held against dissolution, such as Chainfield, Corebreaker, Rift-Hammer, Fortress-Jaw, Skybinder, Veilcrusher, Black Bastion, Dominion-Fist, or Horizon-Torn. A Zakariss who loses a fortress, dissolves under pressure, or is defeated publicly may have part of their name stripped away.
Life in Orimond: In Orimond, zakariss most often become warlords, fortress-builders, Veil conquerors, shock troops, siege-breakers, phase-hunters, spell-renders, and commanders of unstable dominions. Some become core-takers, hunting the resources needed to stabilize their drifting fortresses. Others specialize in breaking teleportation, countering phase-shifts, or anchoring hostile terrain long enough for armies to cross. In mixed realms, they are feared as mercenaries, living weapons, and dangerous specialists against Veil phenomena. They are rarely trusted, but often hired when the problem is too large, too unstable, or too violent for subtler peoples to solve. They usually live near the workplaces, archives, roads, courts, clinics, fortifications, or workshops that support those duties.
Playstyle & Roleplaying: A Zakariss character brings training shaped by territorial control, military strength, stable fortifications, and enforced obligations. Decide which household, order, route, office, or settlement taught you, what obligation still follows you, and what practical problem forced you into the current adventure.
Age: Zakariss reach adulthood around 30 years and commonly live 250 to 400 years, though instability and warfare kill many earlier. Visible aging varies by lineage, health, environment, and occupation.
Size Summary: Zakariss typically stand between 7'0" and 9'0". Their size is Medium.
CharGen: A soul fallen from the stars piloting a chosen vessel and seeking meaning without full memories of a prior age.

Name: Zhimeus
Intro: The Zhimeus are the alchemists of blooming rot, an ancient people shaped by the toxic abundance of Nhal-Zhym. They are kin to older graceful lineages, but centuries of spore-choked air, fungal medicine, and controlled decay have refined them into something stranger and more resilient. Where others see rot as ruin, the Zhimeus see process: a body becoming material, poison becoming cure, infection becoming knowledge, and death becoming a reagent in the next transformation. They do not fear decomposition. They study it, cultivate it, and make it useful.
Origin: Zhimeus descend from Aestari. The Zhimeus arose in Nhal-Zhym, a dangerous land of fungal hollows, toxic wetlands, rot-fields, medicinal molds, and alchemical gardens where survival required more than resistance. It required understanding. Their ancestors adapted slowly to the region’s spores and poisons, learning to filter, ingest, refine, and repurpose what would kill most other peoples. Over generations, this intimacy with decay transformed their bodies, customs, and economy. The Zhimeus became masters of controlled entropy, producing elixirs, antidotes, stimulants, venoms, regenerative salves, and biological wonders from substances no other people could safely touch.
Appearance: Zhimeus are medium humanoids, typically standing 5'7" to 6'3", with a tall, slender, and slightly warped build. They have a long and refined skull, pronounced and ridged brows, a human-like nose, and a narrow jaw. Their 2 deep-set and luminous eyes are commonly pale luminous, silver, spore-flecked. Their skin is fungal, metallic, or mineral-stained, with mushroom-grey, tarnished gold, or verdigris coloration and spore dust, fungal staining, and fine facial veins. Hair is thin, filament-like, or mycelial and they have long pointed ears. They have 2 arms and 2 legs, with long-limbed, five-fingered hands, and plantigrade feet. Their posture is relaxed and deliberate. Typical grooming: Filament or mycelial hair worn loose, dusted, braided, or cultivated with controlled growth. Common ornamentation includes alchemical pendants, fungal materials, reagent vessels, bonework, and craft-earned titles.
Culture & Identity: Zhimeus culture centers on refinement through decay. They believe nothing should be wasted, because all matter is always becoming useful to something else. Corpses, failed crops, spoiled food, infection, venom, and blight are handled with caution, but also with respect as materials in transition. Their society prizes patience, containment, precision, and experimental courage. Sporeweavers study toxins and living chemistry, Rotpriests commune with fungal networks and decomposing matter, while Bonecarvers transform remains into reagents, tools, medicine, or ritual objects. To the Zhimeus, cleanliness means balance and control, not sterility.
Societal Roles: Zhimeus often become alchemists, herbalists, poison-handlers, plague wardens, apothecaries, fungal cultivators, rot-chamber keepers, corpse refiners, antidote makers, and makers of rare medicines. Their expertise is indispensable wherever disease, toxin, blight, or contamination threatens ordinary methods. They also supply regulated elixirs, stimulants, anesthetics, biological catalysts, and dangerous reagents to cities, armies, temples, and mage colleges. Outsiders often fear their work, but those same outsiders come begging when a poison has no name, a wound refuses to close, or a field must be saved by understanding the rot within it.
Naming Conventions: Zhimeus names often reflect ancestry, craft, spore-line, or alchemical discipline. Given names are elegant but slightly sharp in sound, such as Veythis, Xorlan, Thalrin, Jevrak, Mornis, Ithra, Zelis, Xythea, Lirnath, Qemaya, Voriss, and Nhalira. Lineage names may preserve older houses or adapted bloodlines, such as Vhryzhal, Omberis, Kelrith, Nyzareth, Velmyr, and Qorathis. Profession-names and earned titles are common, including Sporeweaver, Rotbinder, Bloomwright, Decayforged, Mold-Sage, Venomglass, Gravebloom, and Verdigris-Hand. A Zhimeus may add a title after developing a cure, surviving a mutation, mastering a rot-field, or refining a substance previously thought unusable.
Life in Orimond: In Orimond, Zhimeus are closely tied to Nhal-Zhym and the trade routes that carry its medicines, toxins, spores, and reagents into the wider world. Their enclaves are carefully regulated, humid, sealed, and thick with the scent of earth, vinegar, medicinal smoke, and living fungus. Outsiders are often quarantined before entering Zhimeus districts, less from hostility than from practical concern. Beyond Nhal-Zhym, Zhimeus are valued as apothecaries, plague consultants, battlefield medics, poison experts, and alchemical specialists, though many communities treat them with suspicion until danger makes their knowledge necessary.
Playstyle & Roleplaying: As a Zhimeus, you bring precision, patience, controlled danger, and the philosophy that decay is transformation rather than failure. Decide what your character refines: poison, medicine, corpses, memory, fungal life, mutation, or the line between healing and harm. You may be calm, clinical, nurturing, unsettling, curious, devout, or dangerously experimental. Zhimeus fit naturally as artificers, druids, wizards, clerics, rogues, rangers, and any character focused on alchemy, toxins, medicine, fungi, death rites, or biological transformation. You excel at knowing that what others discard may be the most powerful ingredient in the room.
Age: Zhimeus reach adulthood around 80 years and commonly live 500 to 700 years. Visible aging varies by lineage, health, environment, and occupation.
Alignement: Zhimeus tend toward Neutral alignment. They value balance, process, and consequence over rigid morality, though individuals may lean toward good or darker pragmatism depending on philosophy.
Size Summary: Zhimeus typically stand between 5'7" and 6'3". Their size is Medium.
Trait 1: Withering Adaptation|You have advantage on saving throws against poison and disease, and you have resistance to necrotic damage.
Trait 2: Cultivated Decay|You gain proficiency with alchemist's supplies or a herbalism kit (your choice). You have advantage on Intelligence checks related to alchemy, toxins, fungi, blight, or contaminated environments.
Trait 3: Alchemical Physiology|When you spend Hit Dice during a short rest, you can reroll one Hit Die and use the new result.
Trait 4: Sporesight|You can sense the presence of disease, poison, rot, or unnatural decay within 30 feet, but not its exact source.
Trait 5: Echoes of the Aestari|You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed.
Trait 6: Refined Entropy|Once per long rest, you can ignore disadvantage imposed by environmental corruption or blight on one ability check or saving throw you make.
CharGen: A tall luminous humanoid with skin locked into a single radiant hue, star-lit eyes, and subtle chromatic glow reflecting a chosen celestial aspect.

Name: Zyrrkhal
Intro: Zyrrkhal are medium people descended from Vorthirith. Their communities are known for durable institutions, exact records, formal law, and continuity of office. Their unusual physical traits are treated as inherited anatomy, training concerns, or medical needs rather than as symbols detached from daily life.
Origin: Zyrrkhal descend from Vorthirith communities that occupied Maligath's oldest citadels, archives, and sealed causeways. Their bodies became stone-like and extremely slow-aging over generations. Descendance is recorded through office inheritance, legal archives, building plans, and unbroken service to specific institutions.
Appearance: Zyrrkhal are medium humanoids, typically standing 6'2" to 7'0", with a very tall, rigid, and sculpted build. They have a severe, elongated, and architectural skull, sharp, severe, and architectural brows, a narrow and sculpted nose, and a long and severe jaw. Their 2 fixed glass or gemstone eyes are commonly obsidian, silver, dark gemstone. Their skin is smooth stone, ceramic, or dark metal, with cold ivory, ash-grey, basalt, or black marble coloration and carved geometric seams and architectural panels. Hair is absent, they bear crown-like cranial architecture, and their secondary covering consists of sculpted stone or ceramic plates. They have 2 arms and 2 legs, with humanoid proportions, five-fingered hands, and plantigrade feet. Their posture is perfectly rigid. Typical grooming: Stone or ceramic surfaces and carved seams kept immaculate and unchanged. Common ornamentation includes architectural robes, geometric panels, office regalia, and symbols of unbroken law.
Culture & Identity: Zyrrkhal culture centers on durable institutions, exact records, formal law, and continuity of office. Children are trained through households, work groups, schools, or orders that teach the habits needed for their expected duties. Status is usually tied to reliable service, visible competence, and whether a person protects the community's records, routes, property, or people.
Societal Roles: Zyrrkhal commonly serve as archivists, magistrates, citadel wardens, oath judges, structural advisors, treaty witnesses, rulers, ceremonial guardians, and keepers of Maligathan law. They are hired when a record, prison, treaty, building, or office must be maintained without interruption.
Naming Conventions: Zyrrkhal names are formal and stable. Given names include Zyrrath, Kholvren, Azkhyr, Tholm, Veyrkal, Ozhant, Krythis, Malzhor, Iskaryn, and Vhalos. Lineage names reference offices, citadels, stone, crowns, or long-held legal duties, and titles often matter more than personal names.
Life in Orimond: In Orimond, Zyrrkhal work in archives, courts, old embassies, fortified offices, treaty halls, and sealed sites. Institutions value their accurate memory and patience, while reformers often find their attachment to old procedure difficult to overcome.
Playstyle & Roleplaying: A Zyrrkhal character brings training shaped by durable institutions, exact records, formal law, and continuity of office. Decide which household, order, route, office, or settlement taught you, what obligation still follows you, and what practical problem forced you into the current adventure.
Age: Zyrrkhal reach adulthood around 50 years and commonly live 800 years or more, with some surviving for several millennia. Visible aging varies by lineage, health, environment, and occupation.
Size Summary: Zyrrkhal typically stand between 6'2" and 7'0". Their size is Medium.
